description: Geography concept progression map — Knowledge and Understanding, Year 7 to 10. Columns are ACARA topic units. Standards within each unit are parallel aspects not sequential prerequisites.
subject: Geography

_notes.column_model: Columns are topic units, not year levels. Standards within a unit are parallel aspects of the same topic.

standards.title: Water as an environmental resource
standards.description: classification of environmental resources and the way that water connects and changes places as it moves through environments
standards.y_goal: Students explain how water is classified as an environmental resource and how it connects and changes places.

standards.title: Water distribution and sustainability
standards.description: the location and distribution of water resources in Australia, their implications, and strategies to manage the sustainability of water
standards.y_goal: Students explain the location and distribution of water resources in Australia and strategies to manage water sustainability.

standards.title: Values of water
standards.description: the economic, cultural, spiritual and aesthetic value of water for people, including First Nations Australians
standards.y_goal: Students explain the economic, cultural, spiritual and aesthetic value of water for people including First Nations Australians.

standards.title: Hydrological and atmospheric hazards
standards.description: the causes and impacts of an atmospheric or hydrological hazard, and responses from communities and governments
standards.y_goal: Students explain the causes and impacts of an atmospheric or hydrological hazard and responses from communities and governments.

standards.title: Liveability decisions
standards.description: factors that influence the decisions people make about where to live, including perceptions of the liveability of places and the influence of environmental quality
standards.y_goal: Students explain factors that influence where people live including perceptions of liveability and environmental quality.

standards.title: Services, facilities and liveability
standards.description: the location and distribution of services and facilities, and implications for liveability of places
standards.y_goal: Students explain the location and distribution of services and facilities and their implications for liveability.

standards.title: Cultural connectedness to place
standards.description: the cultural connectedness of people to places and how this influences their identity, sense of belonging and perceptions of a place, in particular the cultural connectedness of First Nations Australians to Country/Place
standards.y_goal: Students explain how cultural connectedness to places influences identity, belonging and perceptions, especially for First Nations Australians.

standards.title: Strategies for liveability
standards.description: strategies used to enhance the liveability of a place, including for young people, the aged or those with disability, drawing on studies such as those from Australia or Europe
standards.y_goal: Students explain strategies used to enhance the liveability of a place for different groups in the community.

standards.title: Geomorphological processes
standards.description: geomorphological processes that produce different landscapes and significant landforms
standards.y_goal: Students explain geomorphological processes that produce different landscapes and significant landforms.

standards.title: Landscape distribution
standards.description: the location and distribution of Australia's distinctive landscapes and significant landforms
standards.y_goal: Students explain the location and distribution of Australia's distinctive landscapes and significant landforms.

standards.title: Values of landscapes
standards.description: the spiritual, aesthetic and cultural value of landscapes and landforms for people, including Country/Place of First Nations Australians
standards.y_goal: Students explain the spiritual, aesthetic and cultural value of landscapes for people including First Nations Australians.

standards.title: Human activity and landscapes
standards.description: the interconnections between human activity and geomorphological processes, and ways of managing distinctive landscapes
standards.y_goal: Students explain the interconnections between human activity and geomorphological processes and ways of managing landscapes.

standards.title: Geomorphological hazards
standards.description: the causes and impacts of a geomorphological hazard on people, places and environments, and the effects of responses
standards.y_goal: Students explain the causes and impacts of a geomorphological hazard and the effects of responses.

standards.title: Urbanisation and its impacts
standards.description: causes of urbanisation and its impacts on places and environments, drawing on a study from a country such as the United States of America, and its implications
standards.y_goal: Students explain the causes of urbanisation and its impacts on places and environments.

standards.title: Urban distribution
standards.description: differences in the distribution of urban settlements and urban concentration in Australia compared with another country such as the United States of America, and their implications
standards.y_goal: Students explain differences in urban settlement distribution and concentration and their implications.

standards.title: Migration and changing nations
standards.description: reasons for, and effects of, internal migration and international migration in Australia, China or other countries
standards.y_goal: Students explain reasons for and effects of internal and international migration.

standards.title: Urban sustainability strategies
standards.description: strategies to manage the sustainability of Australia's changing urban places
standards.y_goal: Students explain strategies to manage the sustainability of Australia's changing urban places.

standards.title: Biome distribution and characteristics
standards.description: the distribution and characteristics of biomes as regions with distinctive climates, soils, vegetation and productivity
standards.y_goal: Students explain the distribution and characteristics of biomes as regions with distinctive climates, soils, vegetation and productivity.

standards.title: Human alteration of biomes
standards.description: the effects on environments of human alteration of biomes to produce food, industrial materials and fibres
standards.y_goal: Students explain the effects on environments of human alteration of biomes.

standards.title: Agricultural productivity factors
standards.description: the environmental, economic and technological factors that impact agricultural productivity, in Australia and a country in Asia
standards.y_goal: Students explain the environmental, economic and technological factors that impact agricultural productivity.

standards.title: Food security challenges
standards.description: challenges to sustainable food production and food security in Australia and appropriate management strategies
standards.y_goal: Students explain challenges to sustainable food production and food security and appropriate management strategies.

standards.title: Transport, technology and connection
standards.description: the ways changing transportation and technologies are used to connect people to services, information and people in other places
standards.y_goal: Students explain how changing transportation and technologies connect people to services, information and other places.

standards.title: Travel choices and place impacts
standards.description: the effects on places of people's travel, recreational, cultural or leisure choices, and the strategies for managing the impacts on these places
standards.y_goal: Students explain the effects on places of travel and leisure choices and strategies for managing these impacts.

standards.title: Trade interconnections
standards.description: the ways that places and people are interconnected with other places through trade in goods and services, at all scales
standards.y_goal: Students explain how places and people are interconnected through trade at different scales.

standards.title: Consumption and global sustainability
standards.description: the impacts of the production and consumption of goods on places throughout the world, and strategies to manage sustainability in these places
standards.y_goal: Students explain the impacts of production and consumption on places and strategies to manage sustainability.

standards.title: Human-induced environmental change
standards.description: the human-induced changes that challenge the sustainability of places and environments
standards.y_goal: Students explain the human-induced changes that challenge the sustainability of places and environments.

standards.title: Environmental worldviews
standards.description: the environmental world views of people and their implications for environmental management
standards.y_goal: Students explain environmental worldviews and their implications for environmental management.

standards.title: First Nations environmental management
standards.description: First Nations Australians' approaches to custodial responsibility and environmental management in different regions of Australia
standards.y_goal: Students explain First Nations Australians' approaches to custodial responsibility and environmental management.

standards.title: Managing environmental change
standards.description: causes and effects of a change in an identified environment at a local, national or global scale, and strategies to manage sustainability
standards.y_goal: Students explain the causes and effects of environmental change at different scales and strategies to manage sustainability.

standards.title: Measuring human wellbeing
standards.description: the methods used to measure spatial variations in human wellbeing and development, and how these can be applied to determine differences between places at the global scale
standards.y_goal: Students explain methods used to measure spatial variations in human wellbeing and apply them to differences between places.

standards.title: Regional wellbeing variation
standards.description: reasons for, and consequences of, spatial variations in human wellbeing at a regional and national scale, drawing on studies such as from within India or another country in Asia
standards.y_goal: Students explain reasons for and consequences of spatial variations in human wellbeing at regional and national scales.

standards.title: Wellbeing variation in Australia
standards.description: reasons for, and consequences of, spatial variations in human wellbeing in Australia, including for First Nations Australians
standards.y_goal: Students explain reasons for and consequences of spatial variations in human wellbeing in Australia including for First Nations Australians.

standards.title: Responses to improve wellbeing
standards.description: responses of international and national government and non-government organisations to improve human wellbeing in Australia, within India and another country in the Pacific
standards.y_goal: Students explain responses of international and national organisations to improve human wellbeing in different places.

progression_threads.description: Strands follow ACARA topic unit headings. Standards within each unit are parallel aspects not sequential.
